dashboard.quote: This dashboard is how I would track pilot momentum with an executive sponsor: adoption, productivity signals, deal stage, and risks in one view.
The goal is not to declare victory on day one. It is to show what evidence we are collecting to justify scale.
I use this view to make sure security and governance risks are visible before the pilot expands beyond approved repos.

roi.quote: This model is not meant to prove a perfect ROI number on day one. It helps us align on assumptions, instrument the pilot, and define what evidence would justify scaling.
The goal is not to replace developer judgment. It is to reduce time lost to context gathering, boilerplate, test creation, debugging, and review loops.

readiness.quote: The pilot should start with approved repos, SSO, clear data boundaries, model governance, auditability, and rules for where agentic workflows are allowed.
The rollout needs to be manageable at scale, with repo policies, approved integrations, developer enablement, and a feedback loop for what breaks.

pilot.quote: A strong pilot needs business metrics, technical validation, champion development, and CISO alignment before we talk enterprise rollout.
The best pilot will not feel like a top-down productivity mandate. It should show developers where Cursor helps them move faster without giving up control.

meddpicc.quote: MEDDPICC keeps the deal honest: metrics, economic buyer, paper process, champion, and competition all need answers before we forecast with confidence.
I want to see identified pain tied to cost of inaction, not just feature enthusiasm.

workflow.quote: Cursor does not remove developer judgment. It compresses context gathering, boilerplate, test generation, debugging loops, and review prep.
Plan Mode plus Agent gives me a reviewable path before code changes land. I stay in control of the diff.

built.quote: I built this to prove I can learn a technical product quickly and translate it into an enterprise sales motion.
